sourceMovie.title: Chicken Run
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Chicken Run works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chicken Run clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Chicken Run?
faq.answer: Try Moana, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa, The Road to El Dorado—each shares DNA with Chicken Run while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Chicken Run?
faq.answer: Chicken Run (2000) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Chicken Run part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Chicken Run Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
